comment: L56I has been selected in vitro by LEN. It is associated with ~200-fold reduced LEN susceptibility. It has an RC of 3%-9%. It has not been selected in a patient.
N57S had been selected in vitro by GS-CA1 and is associated with ~25-fold reduced GS-CA1 susceptibility.

M66I has been selected in vitro by LEN. It is associated with >1000-fold reduced LEN susceptibility. It has an RC of 1%-6%. It occurred in 6 patients in the CAPELLA study usually in combination with other mutations.

Q67H has been selected in vitro by LEN. It is associated with ~6-fold reduced LEN susceptibility and an RC of ~60%. Q67H+K70R were selected in 2 patients receiving LEN. The combination is associated with nearly 20-fold reduced LEN susceptibility. Q67Y, Q67N, and Q67K have been reported in vitro or in vivo as subdominant variants. Q67Y has been selected in vitro by GS-CA1. It is associated with 10-20-fold reduced LEN acitivity.

The combination is associated with nearly 20-fold reduced LEN susceptibility. Q67Y, Q67N, and Q67K have been reported in vitro or in vivo as subdominant variants. Q67Y has been selected in vitro by GS-CA1. It is associated with 10-20-fold reduced LEN acitivity.

K70HNRS have been selected in patients receiving LEN. K70H alone is associated with greater than 100-fold reduced LEN susceptibility. K70N alone is associated with about 25-fold reduced LEN susceptibility.

K70HNRS have been selected in patients receiving LEN. K70H alone is associated with greater than 100-fold reduced LEN susceptibility. K70N alone is associated with about 25-fold reduced LEN susceptibility.

N74D has been selected in vitro by LEN both alone and in combination with Q67H. It has also been selected alone in four persons receiving LEN including two persons receiving LEN as PrEP. Alone, it is associated with ~20-fold reduced LEN susceptibility and an RC of ~50%. In combination with Q67H, it is associated with >1000 reduced LEN susceptibility.

N74S has been selected in vitro by LEN in combination with Q67H. In combination with Q67H, it is associated with 20-fold reduced susceptibility. N74K has been selected in combination K70H in one person.

N74S has been selected in vitro by LEN in combination with Q67H. In combination with Q67H, it is associated with 20-fold reduced susceptibility. N74K has been selected in combination K70H in one person.
N74T has been selected in one person receiving LEN. Alone, it does not appear to reduce LEN susceptibility.

L10F is a common non-polymorphic, PI-selected accessory mutation associated with reduced in vitro susceptibility to ATV, LPV, and DRV. L10I/V are polymorphic, PI-selected accessory mutations that increase the replication of viruses with other PI-resistance mutations. L10R/Y are rare, non-polymorphic PI-selected mutations. Their effects on PI susceptibility have not been well studied.

L74F is an extremely rare nonpolymorphic mutation. In combination with V75I and either N155H or G140S plus Q148H it reduces susceptibility to DTG and CAB.

L74I is a highly polymorphic variant, occurring in 3%–16% of viruses depending on subtype. It is the consensus amino acid in subtype A6. In contrast to L74M, L74I is not enriched in INSTI-experienced individuals compared with INSTI-naïve individuals. In the ATLAS and FLAIR trials, the presence of subtype A6 and L74I at baseline was associated with a higher risk of VF on long-acting CAB/RPV, with 11/180 (6%) participants experiencing VF. However, it remains unclear whether this risk was driven by L74I, some other aspect of the A6 sequence, or by trial-site factors specific to the regions where subtype A6 viruses predominated. L74I has not been shown to reduce in vitro susceptibility to any integrase inhibitor, either alone or in combination with other DRMs.

V75 is a nonpolymorphic position. V75I has been selected in vitro by EVG and DTG. V75I has been selected in 0.5% to 1% of PWH receiving RAL and rarely in PWH with VF on DTG for whom sequences were available. V75I plus L74F has been reported to reduce susceptibility to DTG and CAB. V75A has been selected in vitro by CAB. V75A has rarely been selected during INSTI therapy and its effect on INSTI susceptibility is not known.

S147G is a nonpolymorphic mutation selected in patients receiving RAL, EVG, and DTG. Alone it reduces EVG susceptibility about 5-fold. It appears to contribute reduced susceptibility to the remaining INSTIs when it occurs in combination with Q148 mutations and N155H.

N155H is a common nonpolymorphic INSTI-resistance mutations. It has been reported in a high proportion of persons developing VF and HIVDR while receiving RAL, EVG, DTG, and CAB. Few data are available for BIC. Alone, it reduces RAL and EVG susceptibility >10-fold while reducing DTG, BIC, and CAB by about 1.5-fold. N155S/T are extremely rare nonpolymorphic mutations that reduce RAL and EVG susceptibility about 5-fold and greater than 30-fold respectively but have little effect on CAB, DTG, and BIC.

E157Q occurs in 1% to 5% of viruses from INSTI-naïve patients depending on subtype. It is selected during INSTI therapy nearly always in combinations with other INSTI-resistance mutations. It has minimal if any effect on INSTI susceptibilities and alone does not appear to influence the response to INSTI therapy.

R263K is a nonpolymorphic mutation selected in vitro by EVG, DTG, BIC, and CAB. It occurs in a high proportion of persons who develop VF and emergent HIVDR while receiving DTG. This mutation typically occurs without other INSTI DRMs and, in this context, it reduces DTG, BIC, and CAB susceptibility about 2-fold.
